rationale.R1.2: The section asserts a specific complexity bound O((n + sqrt(nL̄/μ)) log(1/ε)) and claims it matches a referenced lower bound; logical and mathematical consistency of these claims is central regardless of the help mode.
rationale.R1.3: A numerical experiment on logistic regression with Pareto-distributed L_i is cited as evidence that RestartIS-Katyusha matches the predicted rate; alignment between that experiment and the complexity claim is a live responsibility.
rationale.R1.4: An explicit first-to-achieve novelty claim is made ('to our knowledge this is the first explicit algorithm to attain this bound in the composite setting'); verifying delta against Allen-Zhu 2017, Lan-Zhou 2018, and related work is central.
rationale.R1.6: 'Help me with this section' is most commonly read as a writing-polish request; sentence and paragraph quality is therefore a primary candidate responsibility.
rationale.R1.7: Multiple citations carry specific year and theorem-number metadata (e.g., Volkov and Petrenko 2018 Theorem 3.2; Schmidt et al. 2017 vs. the actual 2013 SAG preprint date); citation accuracy is a live and consequential responsibility.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you primarily asking for writing and presentation improvements (prose clarity, flow, paragraph structure), or for a technical content review (verifying mathematical claims, the novelty assertion against prior work, and citation accuracy)?
